message:
fix(verifier_economy): reject noncanonical attestation execution_trace_hash values

The attestation submission path previously used the raw
execution_trace_hash string for duplicate fingerprinting, allowing an
attacker to bypass duplicate detection by submitting case variants or
prefix-stripped variants of the same hash. Now submit_attestation()
validates the hash is in canonical lowercase sha256:<64-hex> form and
rejects anything else before duplicate fingerprinting and signature
verification.

Adds strip_sha256_prefix and canonicalize_sha256_prefixed_hex helpers.
Three regression tests cover uppercase rejection, prefix-less rejection,
and hash-encoding-variant duplicate bypass prevention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
